title: heracleum pubescens var. wilhelmsii
intro: Heracleum pubescens var. wilhelmsii, commonly known as the wild angelica or cow parsley, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of wild angelica include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system. Wild angelica can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or ointment, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies, such as treating colds, flu, and skin conditions.
